title: Phase 4: Publish via Changesets, remove semantic-release

Add cache-port (injectable revalidate) + web setRevalidate adapter. Keep 'use cache' reads in app. Verify grep next/ in packages/core/src empty + tests green.

Rewrote 30 @/schema imports to @repo/db; db:* scripts moved to the package, root delegates --filter @repo/db, apps/web vercel-build migrates via @repo/db. Verified: typecheck (both), no schema drift, tests, build.

Verify turbo run build test typecheck green and FULL TURBO on second run.

title: Price OpenAI fast-mode (priority tier) gpt-5.5 usage
description: models.dev only carries the standard gpt-5.5 rate, so fast-mode (priority tier) usage is under-priced. Add a provider-scoped PRIORITY_RATES override in pricing.ts with OpenAI's published priority rate (input $12.50/M, cached $1.25/M, output $75/M = 2.5x standard) and return it from priceFor before the models.dev lookup. Bake into existing costUsd; no schema/PK/display changes. Per-model always-priority config.
notes: Override keyed openai->gpt-5.5-fast (the actual OpenCode model id for the fast tier), NOT plain gpt-5.5. Plain gpt-5.5 stays on standard models.dev rate. Verified via re-ingest: gpt-5.5 openai = $1029.02 (standard), gpt-5.5-fast openai = $3439.60 (priority 2.5x). N.A. rows dropped 37->13. 7 pricing tests pass; typecheck + lint clean on changed files.
close_reason: Shipped in a8df0fa: PRIORITY_RATES override prices openai/gpt-5.5-fast at the priority tier (2.5x); plain gpt-5.5 stays standard. Verified end-to-end via re-ingest.

Optional enhancement to usage ingestion: local CLI parses ~/.claude + ~/.codex (must stay local), then POSTs daily aggregates to an authed API route that start()s a Vercel Workflow to durably price -> upsert -> revalidate the /usage cache. Adds retries/observability on the cloud half. Parsing cannot move to the cloud (Workflow steps run on Vercel, no local fs). Blocked-by/after the local CLI ingestion (blog-4yt).

title: Integrate WakaTime AI activity stats
description:
## Context

WakaTime has an AI Overview dashboard showing AI-related coding metrics such as AI-attributed lines, AI vs human changes, prompt counts, token usage, and follow-up rates. We want to KIV integrating a documented subset of these stats into the site dashboard.

## Scope

Investigate and implement WakaTime AI activity stats using documented APIs only. The documented WakaTime API appears to expose AI activity indirectly through heartbeats at GET /api/v1/users/current/heartbeats?date=YYYY-MM-DD. Heartbeats may include category: "ai coding", line_additions, and line_deletions.

## Supported Metrics

This can support AI-attributed lines, human-attributed lines, AI additions/deletions, human additions/deletions, and an AI vs Human by day chart.

## Out Of Scope

Input tokens, output tokens, AI prompts, average prompt length, human follow-up rate, and human follow-up edits are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official documented API for them.

## Implementation Direction

Add a server-only WakaTime client in src/lib/wakatime.ts. Add WAKATIME_API_KEY to environment configuration. Aggregate heartbeats over a configurable date range. Display summary cards in /dashboard. Optionally add a Recharts bar chart for AI vs Human by day. Avoid undocumented dashboard or private endpoints.
acceptance_criteria:
WakaTime API key is never exposed client-side.
Missing API key fails gracefully.
Dashboard can show AI vs human line-change stats when data is available.
Token and prompt metrics are excluded unless WakaTime exposes an official API.
Beads issue is linked or synced with the GitHub issue.
